document: Introduction to Internet of Things Assignment-Week 7 Solutions

questions.questionText: In the context of networking with Raspberry Pi, what is the purpose of the socket.bind() function on the server side?
options: a. To connect to a remote server
b. To associate the socket with a specific network interface and port number
c. To listen for incoming connections
d. To close the socket connection
questions.explanation: The bind() function binds the socket to the address (host name) and the port number specified in the arguments. (See lecture 31 @ 08:27)

questions.questionText: Which Python library is used to integrate the DHT sensor with Raspberry Pi?
questions.explanation: The Adafruit library is used to integrate the DHT sensor to Raspberry Pi via Python framework. (See lecture 31 @ 04:30)

questions.questionText: In the client-server architecture demonstrated, what type of socket is used for data transmission?
questions.explanation: The lecture demonstrates creating a UDP based socket which will send data from client to the server. (See lecture 31 @ 05:44)

questions.questionText: Which function is used in Python to split a string into a list of substrings based on a delimiter?
questions.explanation: The split() function can be used to split a string into multiple strings depending on the separator or delimiter. (See lecture 32@ 08:06)

questions.questionText: Which one of the following Python libraries is used for data plotting?
questions.explanation: Python supports a very powerful library called matplotlib for plotting. (See lecture 32 @ 11:52)

questions.questionText: Which function in the plotting library is used to enable the interactive plot mode?
questions.explanation: ion is the interactive plot mode, which is generally used for animations. (See lecture 32 @ 23:23)

questions.questionText: The interface between the Control Plane and the Data Plane in SDN is known as:
questions.explanation: The interface between the forwarding (data plane) and the control plane is known as the Southbound API. (See lecture 33 @ 14:18)

questions.questionText: Flow tables in SDN switches are typically stored in which type of specialized memory?
questions.explanation: Flow tables are stored in a specialized memory known as TCAM (Ternary Content Addressable Memory). (See lecture 33@ 21:07)

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT an attribute or component of SDN?
questions.explanation: Attributes or components of SDN includes hardware switches, controllers, applications, flow rules, application programming interfaces (APIs) (See lecture 33 @ 15:40)

questions.questionText: Which API terminology is used when referring to communication between multiple controllers in the control layer?
questions.explanation: The East-Westbound API terminology comes into the picture when talking about not a single controller but multiple controllers in the control layer. (See lecture 34 @ 02:27)

questions.questionText: What is the typical delay involved in new rule placement if the rule is not already present in the switch's flow table?
questions.explanation: Typically there is a delay of 3 to 5 milliseconds involved in the new rule placement. (See lecture 34 @ 03:40)

questions.questionText: Approximately how many requests per second can a typical single-threaded SDN controller handle?
questions.explanation: Typically a controller can handle roughly about 200 requests in a second (applicable for single-threaded applications). (See lecture 34 @ 04:13)

options: a. A hardware component for sensors
b. A Software Defined WSN platform where the flow table is available at sensor nodes
d. A type of cloud storage
questions.explanation: SDN-WISE is a Stateful Software Defined solution for Wireless Sensor Networks where flow tables for rule placement are available at the sensor nodes. (See lecture 35 @ 13:49)

questions.questionText: In the experimental results shown for Soft-WSN (Software Defined Wireless Sensor Network), how does the Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) compare to a traditional WSN?
options: a. PDR is lower in Soft-WSN
b. PDR is significantly improved (higher) in Soft-WSN
c. PDR remains exactly the same
d. Soft-WSN does not support packet delivery
questions.explanation: Experimental results show that the network performance, specifically Packet Delivery Ratio, is improved using Soft-WSN over traditional WSN. (See lecture 35 @ 12:00)

questions.questionText: In the "Software Defined IoT" architecture, where are flow classification and enhanced security typically handled?
options: a. At the physical sensor nodes
b. At the Data Center Networks
d. At the user's mobile device
questions.explanation: Flow classification and enhanced security are taken care of at the data center networks in the SDN-IoT architecture. (See lecture 35 @ 06:59)
